mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with rising inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force central banks to tighten, resulting in negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise simultaneously, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below -0.5 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals a supply-side contraction that outweighs rate effects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations during tightening periods when INDPRO declines, invalidating the supply contraction channel.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day returns are negative in >60% of payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.1 million units, downstream construction spending and consumer confidence deteriorate, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of cases when HOUST drops below 1.1 million, or the threshold shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising unemployment indicate a liquidity trap where monetary easing fails to stimulate growth, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive risk-adjusted return over 5-year walk-forward tests, or the signal fails to separate from baseline market drift.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth during a low-inflation regime confirms sustainable economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations when GDPC1 rises in low-inflation periods, or the signal loses significance after controlling for market beta.
